reasoning: The informal statement says "Let K be a finite field; the product of nonzero elements is -1." The Lean statement uses [Field K] [Fintype Kˣ], which is equivalent to "K is a finite field" because for a field, K = Kˣ ∪ {0}, so Fintype Kˣ ↔ Fintype K. The nonzero elements of a field are precisely the units, so ∏ x : Kˣ, x captures the product of nonzero elements. The equality is taken in Kˣ, but since the coercion Kˣ → K is injective and sends -1 to -1 (Units.coe_neg_one), this is logically equivalent to the informal claim in K. This is essentially Mathlib's own formulation in prod_univ_units_id_eq_neg_one (FieldTheory/Finite/Basic.lean line 110), which uses the same [Fintype Kˣ] approach to avoid typeclass diamonds. The formalization is strictly faithful.
